_comment:
Aspect catalog. One registry, three sections — same code shape for all entries (the `category` recorded on each Aspect record reflects the section it was parsed under). Sub-aspect nesting is preserved within a section: a card carrying `pine` satisfies `aspect.wood.min: 1` because pine is nested under wood. Sub-aspects inherit `icon`, `color`, `description` and `category` from the nearest declaring ancestor — omit a field (or leave description as "") to inherit. `icon` and `color` are OPTIONAL throughout (default `""` and `0x888888`) — entries with no display intent (most traits) just leave them off. The three sections:

  • `aspects` — WHAT a card has. Resources, materials, soul stats. Primary recipe inputs. Displayed in details (always, including the minimum/collapsed state).
  • `features` — HOW a card acts. Behavioural / functional tags (faction, fleeting, level, crafting, speed, inventory). Recipe-matchable too, just less commonly. Displayed in details only when expanded; rendered above the description in a smaller pip row.
  • `traits` — WHAT a card is. Descriptive properties read by simulation code (movement cost, height, climate envelopes, placement metadata). Not displayed. Recipe-matchable via the same `aspect.<name>.min: N` predicate (values are f32 so float predicates work).

Values on cards are f32 (the same value can be `2`, `2.0`, or `1.2` — author's choice; integers parse without loss).

flora.description: Living plant matter — trees, bushes, undergrowth

corpse.description: Remains — a spent body, suitable as a recipe input for rituals and similar story beats

corpus--.description: Injuries — wounds, broken bones, lasting harm

corpus.description: The body — physical capacity and health

crafting.description: Construction and fabrication — building, shaping, assembling

level.description: Tier / rank — used as a render index by cards whose object pack stores one variant per level (e.g. the alter tile).

faction.description: Faction allegiance — sub-features pick a specific faction folder. A card carrying any descendant of `faction` renders with that descendant's folder (e.g. `master/<object>/chorus/`) instead of the local player's faction. Lets a designer ship faction-specific cards that look the same regardless of who holds them.

speed.description: Movement allowance per turn. Spent against tile `cost` when the soul traverses the map.

inventory.description: Has an inventory — click the card to open the bag of cards owned by it.

cost.description: Movement cost paid to enter the tile. Higher values represent rougher or slower terrain.

height.description: Spatial elevation of the tile — affects lighting, reach, and terrain layering. Per-tile, not summed across a chain.

elevation_min.description: Lower bound (inclusive) of the elevation envelope. [0, 1]. Cells whose sampled elevation falls below this exclude the tile from the candidate set. Omitted ⇒ 0 (no lower bound).

elevation_max.description: Upper bound (inclusive) of the elevation envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1 (no upper bound).

temperature_min.description: Lower bound (inclusive) of the temperature envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = tundra, 1 = desert. Omitted ⇒ 0.

temperature_max.description: Upper bound (inclusive) of the temperature envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.

humidity_min.description: Lower bound (inclusive) of the humidity envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = arid, 1 = saturated. Omitted ⇒ 0.

humidity_max.description: Upper bound (inclusive) of the humidity envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.

aether_min.description: Lower bound (inclusive) of the aether-saturation envelope. [0, 1]. 0 = mundane, 1 = ley-line / resonance site. Omitted ⇒ 0.

aether_max.description: Upper bound (inclusive) of the aether-saturation envelope. [0, 1]. Omitted ⇒ 1.

rarity.description: Relative weight when multiple tiles share a cell's candidate set. Default 1.0.

coverage.description: Hex coverage role. 0 = decorator overlay, 1 = base tile (fills the hex). Read as boolean (`>= 0.5` is base). Omitted ⇒ 1.

cluster_group.description: Cluster group id — tiles sharing a non-zero value bias toward spawning adjacent to one another. 0 = no clustering (default).

cluster_strength.description: How much the cluster bias overrides the climate-only candidate roll. [0, 1]. 0 = clustering ignored; 1 = if any neighbor shares this group, this tile wins. Omitted ⇒ 0.
